steps.title: Set up the probability formula
steps.content:
For a random selection from the 185 animals, the probability of choosing a species-Y animal is:
$$P(\text{species Y}) = \frac{\text{number of species Y animals}}{\text{total animals}} = \frac{\text{(species Y count)}}{185}$$
The denominator is fixed at 185; the numerator comes from the frequency table.

steps.title: Read species Y from the figure and match
steps.content:
From the frequency table, the species Y count is 3, so:
$$P(\text{species Y}) = \frac{3}{185}$$
Checking the choices: $\frac{70}{185}$ and $\frac{112}{185}$ would correspond to much larger counts, and $\frac{182}{185}$ would mean species Y makes up nearly all 185 animals. Only $\frac{3}{185}$ matches a species-Y count of 3. That matches choice B — B is correct.
